title: Order Your First Coffee
situation: You walk into a small coffee shop in London on a rainy morning. The barista smiles and waits for your order at the counter.

title: Lost in the City
situation: You are a tourist in New York and need directions to Times Square. You stop a friendly local at a crosswalk to ask for help.

title: Greet a New Coworker
situation: It is your first day in a small office. A friendly coworker walks up to your desk to say hello and ask your name.

title: At the Grocery Store
situation: You are at a small neighborhood grocery store. You bring your basket to the cashier to pay for milk, bread, and eggs.

title: At the Pharmacy
situation: You have a small headache and walk into a pharmacy to ask for simple medicine. The pharmacist greets you at the counter.

title: Quick Call Home
situation: You call your mom for a short check-in. She picks up and asks how you are doing today.

title: Meet Your New Neighbor
situation: A neighbor from the apartment next door knocks to welcome you to the building and introduce themselves.

title: Book a Doctor Visit
situation: You call a clinic to book a general check-up for next week. The receptionist answers and asks about your availability.

title: Take a Taxi
situation: You step into a taxi after a long flight and need to give the driver the address of your hotel and confirm the fare.

title: Coffee Break Chat
situation: A coworker joins you in the office kitchen during the morning coffee break and starts a small chat about the weekend.

title: Sign Up for a Gym
situation: You walk into a local gym to ask about a monthly membership. A staff member at the front desk greets you and explains the options.

title: First Message Back
situation: You matched with someone on a dating app yesterday. They just sent a friendly first message asking about your hobbies and weekend plans.

title: First Day at the Office
situation: Your new manager stops by your desk on your first day to welcome you, ask how your morning went, and set expectations for the first week.

title: Join the Hiking Club
situation: You show up at a weekend hiking meetup in the park and introduce yourself to the group leader before the walk starts.

title: Call Your Grandparents
situation: You video call your grandparents to share what you did last weekend and ask how they have been.

title: Lost Luggage at the Airport
situation: Your suitcase did not arrive on the carousel after a long international flight. You walk over to the airline's lost-baggage counter to file a claim.

title: Toothache at the Dentist
situation: You have a sharp toothache and walk into a dental clinic without an appointment. The dentist asks where the pain is and how long it has been going on.

title: Coffee With a Match
situation: You meet someone for the first time at a café after matching on a dating app. They arrive a little early and wave you over.

title: Hotel Reservation Mix-up
situation: You arrive at a hotel close to midnight to check in and learn your room was given to another guest by mistake. You need to sort it out calmly.

title: Push Back in Stand-up
situation: In a morning stand-up, a teammate proposes a plan you think is risky. You want to push back politely and suggest a safer alternative.

title: Asking for a Second Opinion
situation: You sit down with a new doctor to ask for a second opinion on a treatment plan another doctor proposed. You want to explain your worries clearly.

title: A Difficult Family Talk
situation: Your sibling made a decision that affects the whole family. You want to share your concerns honestly without starting a fight.

title: Networking Night
situation: You are at an industry mixer and strike up a conversation with a senior professional at the bar. They ask what you have been working on lately.

title: Negotiate Your Raise
situation: You sit down with your manager for your year-end review to make the case for a raise, backed by your impact over the last twelve months.

title: An Honest Talk
situation: You are a few months into dating someone and want to bring up where the relationship is going. They sit down across from you, sensing something is on your mind.

title: Complain to the Manager
situation: Your weekend stay was ruined by noise, dirty linens, and a broken air conditioner. You ask to speak with the hotel manager to lay out the issues and request fair compensation.

title: Defend Your Take
situation: At a book club meeting, your reading of the novel disagrees with the host's. You want to make your case clearly and listen well to theirs.

title: Pre-Keynote Pep Talk
situation: Five minutes before delivering a keynote, your senior advisor pulls you aside backstage to challenge your framing and push you to sharpen the closing argument.

title: On the Panel
situation: You are a panelist at a high-profile conference. The moderator throws a sharp, contested question at you in front of a packed auditorium and a live stream.

title: The Future Together
situation: After two years of long-distance dating, you sit down with your partner to talk through whether one of you should move, and what you each truly want from the next chapter.

title: Family Legacy Talk
situation: Your aging parent calls a family meeting to discuss the will, the family business, and how responsibilities should be shared. Tensions sit just under the surface.

title: Discussing a Diagnosis
situation: A specialist sits down with you to walk through a complex diagnosis, treatment options, and the trade-offs of each path. You need to ask precise, informed questions.
